title: What Is a Program?
subtitle: A file with instructions the computer follows

sections.content: A **program** is a file that contains instructions the computer can execute (run). Every app on your computer — Chrome, Spotify, Calculator — is a program.
sections.analogy: A program is like a recipe. The recipe (code) sits in a file. When you "run" it, the computer follows the instructions step by step, just like a chef follows a recipe.
keyPoints: Every app on your computer is a program: Chrome, Spotify, Calculator
A program starts as code (text in a file) written by a developer
When you "run" a program, the computer reads the instructions and executes them
When you double-click an app icon, you're telling the computer: "read this program's files and execute the instructions"

sections.instruction: This is a simple program written in plain English. Click "Run Program" to watch the computer execute it step by step. When it asks for your name, type it in!
lines: Ask the user for their name

sections.question: What is a program?
options: A file containing instructions that the computer can execute
A special folder on your computer
A website you visit in a browser
sections.explanation: A program is a file (or set of files) with instructions. When you run it, the computer follows those instructions to do something useful.

sections.question: When you double-click the Chrome icon on your desktop, what happens?
options: It downloads Chrome from the internet
The computer reads Chrome's program files and runs the instructions
It creates a new file on your desktop
Nothing happens until you type a URL
sections.explanation: Double-clicking an app icon tells the computer to find that program's files, read the instructions, and execute them. That's why there's sometimes a brief loading moment — the computer is reading and starting up the program.

completionMessage: You now understand what programs are — files with instructions the computer executes. Next, we'll learn about the terminal: a text-based way to give your computer instructions directly.
